mentions.note: Valued at $830B seeking $100B private raise; CFO said no near-term IPO; massive operating losses, $1.4T+ spending commitments; if forced public 2026, huge losses would shock Wall Street; better off raising private capital.

$1.4T compute commitment vs. $20B annual revenue; must raise $100B+ at $800B+ valuation; competition from Gemini (Google) and Claude (Anthropic); Elon Musk lawsuit seeks $134B; biggest risk to AI data center complex in 2026.

Will successfully raise capital across multiple tranches ($10B per gigawatt from Nvidia, private round at $500-800B, future IPO); Nvidia-Altman partnership solid; no controversy despite media negativity.
Raised $110B at $730B valuation; committed $100B to buying Amazon chips; representative of hyperscaler capex arms race.
Pre-IPO company; ChatGPT at $25B+ ARR, up 17% since year-end. Taking share from enterprise software, creating zero-sum disruption.

Closed $120B+ fundraise at $840B+ valuation; 900M weekly ChatGPT users, 50M subscribers, Frontier model shipping April. Force multiplier thesis (workers more productive, not fewer). IPO 2026-2027; retail access underway.

Raised $120B private capital round; no pressure to go public near-term. CFO Sarah Friar says company 'starting to build' IPO outcome. Market conditions too weak; waiting for stabilization before public debut likely.
Private; $852B valuation; upcoming IPO; capital drain risk; will compete with SpaceX and Anthropic for retail capital.
Private/pre-IPO AI leader; Cramer positions it alongside Anthropic as 'kings of creches' alongside hardware users (Amazon, Meta, Google).
Pre-IPO private company; ChatGPT user intent declining vs. Claude at all-time high; still positive 10% momentum but losing head-to-head; rumored to deploy big with next software iteration.
IPO risk; jury selection began in Musk lawsuit today. If resolved, could IPO at ~$1T valuation, sucking capital from rest of market. Currently private.
WSJ article raised concerns but CFO Sarah Fryer trusted; not a Lucent/Nortel-style Ponzi; not paid to bet against it in practice or theory; real risks exist but not catastrophic.
Valuation $157B (Oct 2024) → $500B (Oct 2024) → $852B (latest). $122B mega-fundraise. IPO likely 2026. Supply risk similar to SpaceX: hot IPO then painful correction.
Private pre-IPO AI company disrupting traditional enterprise software; alongside Anthropic, proving cost-benefit case for AI over software vendors; #2 on CNBC Disruptor 50.
Listed as key Nvidia client; included alongside Anthropic and SpaceX in Jensen's approved customer tier for next-gen Nvidia infrastructure.
Unprofitable with unclear path to profitability; if it comes last in IPO queue after Anthropic and SpaceX, will face significantly compressed valuations and limited market receptivity.
Feels speculative despite private valuation hype; losing fortunes. Private market bidding up positions on nothing; public market may lack liquidity to absorb exits. Valuation overheated.
10% more-vs-less puts in top 10% of all brands but Cramer worried about competitive position. Anthropic (Claude) is preferred choice with superior user momentum metrics.
Not doing well; has some good people and products but not good balance sheet. Will probably be last in IPO shoot depending on deal terms; have to see where deal comes before jumping in.
